title: GitHub Enterprise Admin
description: Managing a GitHub Enterprise instance requires understanding the org hierarchy, authentication flows, and policy enforcement across the entire estate.

sections.title: SAML / SSO Configuration
sections.description:
GitHub Enterprise supports SAML 2.0 and OIDC for single sign-on. The IdP asserts user identity via signed assertions. GitHub validates the assertion, maps the NameID to a user, and applies SCIM-provisioned team memberships. Configure enterprise/admin/sso to enforce SAML at the enterprise level with Just-in-Time provisioning fallback.

# Example SAML assertion attributes
NameID: user@company.com
AttributeStatement:
  - Name: groups
    Value: [engineering, platform]
  - Name: roles
    Value: admin

sections.title: SCIM Provisioning
sections.description:
System for Cross-domain Identity Management (SCIM) automates user and team lifecycle. When a user is deprovisioned in the IdP, SCIM sends a DELETE to GitHub's SCIM API, removing access within minutes. Enable SCIM at /settings/installed_sso and configure the bearer token in your identity provider.

PATCH /scim/v2/Users/{id}
{
  "active": false,
  "displayName": "John Doe"
}

sections.title: Audit Log
sections.description: The enterprise audit log captures all events across organizations. Stream events to Splunk, Datadog, or Azure Blob Storage via the streaming API. Events include repo.create, org.update, team.add_member, and actions.workflow_run. Query with gh api /enterprises/{ent}/audit-log for custom dashboards.

sections.title: Org Policies
sections.description: Enterprise owners set policies that cascade to all child organizations. Policies include base permissions, repo creation visibility, fork permissions, and Actions allowed IP ranges. Use gh api /enterprises/{ent}/settings/billing and policy enforcement via enterprise/policies in the settings UI or via the GraphQL enterpriseAdministration API.

sections.title: Repository Rulesets
sections.description:
Rulesets allow fine-grained branch and tag protections applied at enterprise, org, or repo level. Each ruleset has an evaluation order (BY_INDEX) and bypass permissions for admins. Deploy rulesets via gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/rulesets or the .github/rulesets.yml file in the .github repository.

name: block-force-push
conditions:
  refName:
    include: ["refs/heads/main"]
rules:
  - type: required_status_check
    parameters:
      required_status_checks: ["CI"]

details: Enterprise administration spans identity, security, billing, and policy. The authentication flow begins when a user visits an org resource — GitHub redirects to the IdP login endpoint, the IdP returns a signed SAML response, GitHub validates the signature and maps attributes to local groups via SCIM. Audit logging captures a full audit trail with actor, action, resource, and IP. For compliance (SOC 2, FedRAMP), enable the audit log streaming to a SIEM with a retention policy of at least 365 days. Repository rulesets are evaluated in order with the first matching ruleset applying; global rulesets apply before org rulesets, which apply before repo rulesets. Use the evaluation API endpoint to dry-run rulesets before enforcement. Billing is aggregated at the enterprise level with per-org breakdown via the GraphQL billingInfo query.
cheatSheet:
Core Commands

  • gh api /enterprises/{ent}/audit-log — query enterprise audit events.
  • gh api /enterprises/{ent}/settings/billing — inspect billing and usage.
  • gh api /orgs/{org}/rulesets — manage repository rulesets at org level.
  • gh api /repos/{owner}/{repo}/rulesets — manage repo-level rulesets.

Staff+ Patterns

  • SCIM + SAML together create a fully automated identity lifecycle — deprovisioning in the IdP removes GitHub access within minutes.
  • Audit log streaming to a SIEM (Splunk, Datadog) with 365-day retention satisfies SOC 2 / FedRAMP.
  • Rulesets cascade: enterprise → org → repo. Use evaluate mode before enforcing to test impact.

Watch Outs

  • SAML Just-in-Time provisioning without SCIM leaves orphaned accounts when users leave the IdP.
  • Rulesets in BY_INDEX order — first match wins. Place global rules before org rules.
  • Bypass lists for admins should be intentional, not default. Audit bypass events quarterly.

Interview Angle

GitHub L6: "Design the enterprise admin console for a company with 10K repos and 200 orgs." Discuss hierarchical policy inheritance, SCIM user provisioning at scale, and audit log streaming with sub-second SLAs for high-severity events.
